commands.review_summary:
The branch returns a 100%-used Command Code primary window for depleted unknown-plan snapshots and adds a regression test for the success-to-flaky-to-success notification dedup sequence.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can produce a nil Command Code primary window after a subscription enrichment timeout, and the session quota path clears dedup state on that nil window. I did not run tests because this ClawSweeper review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of a visible compatibility side effect, not a narrow automation-repairable code defect.

target.title: fix(commandcode): stop repeated 'session depleted' notification after flaky subscription fetch
target.body:
Fixes #1624.

## Summary

When a Command Code account is genuinely depleted and the subscription endpoint times out or fails (the 2s grace period from #1521), `makePrimaryWindow()` returned `nil`, which caused `handleSessionQuotaTransition` to clear the session-quota dedup state (`lastKnownSessionRemaining`/`lastKnownSessionWindowSource`). On the next cycle where the subscription succeeded, the depleted state looked like a fresh "startup depleted" transition and re-fired the notification — repeating roughly every refresh cycle (default 1m) while the endpoint was intermittently flaky.

See #1624 for the full root-cause trace (the dedup clearing path at `UsageStore.swift:780-784`, the nil-window trigger in `CommandCodeUsageSnapshot.makePrimaryWindow()`, and why the `previousSource != currentSource` branch at `:790` doesn't catch it).

## Fix

Return a fully-used window (`usedPercent: 100`) instead of `nil` in `makePrimaryWindow()` when the plan is unknown and credits are depleted, so `snapshot.primary` stays non-nil and the dedup state survives subscription flakiness. `makePrimaryWindow()` is the only producer of `snapshot.primary` for Command Code, so this keeps the contract `UsageStore` relies on without touching notifier or fetcher code.

## Behavior changes

This is a visible change, not a no-op. A depleted Command Code account on an unknown plan (either subscription flakiness or genuinely free-tier) goes from "no primary window" to "0%-remaining / fully-used bar" across the menu bar, menu card, dropdown menu, switcher, and widget — with `resetsAt` backfilled from the prior cycle's cached snapshot via `backfillingResetTimes` on the `applyProviderRefreshOutcome` success branch. The fix also stops a parallel re-fire in the quota-warning path (a depleted account on a flaky subscription cycle would otherwise clear `quotaWarningState[key]` and re-fire a "20% left" warning on recovery). The fix affects free-tier-depleted users as a steady-state display change, not just paid users experiencing flakiness.

## Test

Adds a regression test that routes through `CommandCodeUsageSnapshot.toUsageSnapshot()` so `makePrimaryWindow()` is actually exercised (hand-constructing `UsageSnapshot(primary: nil, …)` would bypass the fix). The 3-cycle sequence (success → flaky → success) asserts 1 `.depleted` post with the fix vs 2 without.
